name: astronomer
description: A Pulumi package for creating and managing Astronomer Cloud resources
attribution: This Pulumi package is based on the [`astro` Terraform Provider](https://github.com/astronomer/terraform-provider-astro).

nodejs.packageDescription: A Pulumi package for creating and managing Astronomer Cloud resources. Based on terraform-provider-astro: version vv1.2.3
nodejs.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/astronomer/terraform-provider-astro)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-astronomer` repo](https://github.com/ryan-pip/pulumi-astronomer/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-astro` repo](https://github.com/astronomer/terraform-provider-astro/issues).

python.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/astronomer/terraform-provider-astro)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-astronomer` repo](https://github.com/ryan-pip/pulumi-astronomer/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-astro` repo](https://github.com/astronomer/terraform-provider-astro/issues).

host.description: API host to use for the provider. Default is `https://api.astronomer.io`

organizationId.description: Organization ID this provider will operate on.

token.description: Astro API Token. Can be set with an `ASTRO_API_TOKEN` env var.

deploymentId.description: The deployment ID the notification channel is scoped to

entityId.description: The entity ID the notification channel is scoped to

entityName.description: The name of the entity the notification channel is scoped to

entityType.description: The type of entity the notification channel is scoped to (e.g., 'DEPLOYMENT')

isShared.description: When entity type is scoped to ORGANIZATION or WORKSPACE, this determines if child entities can access this notification channel.

workspaceId.description: The workspace ID the notification channel is scoped to

apiKey.description: The API key for the notification channel

dagId.description: The DAG ID for the notification channel

deploymentApiToken.description: The deployment API token for the notification channel

deploymentId.description: The deployment ID for the notification channel

integrationKey.description: The integration key for the notification channel

recipients.description: The recipients for the notification channel

webhookUrl.description: The webhook URL for the notification channel

patternMatches.description: The alert's pattern matches to match against

properties.description: The alert's properties used to define the alert

entityType.description: The type of entity to match against

operatorType.description: The type of operator to use for the pattern match

dagDeadline.description: The deadline for the DAG in HH:MM 24-hour UTC format

dagDurationSeconds.description: The duration of the DAG in seconds (minimum 60)

daysOfWeeks.description: The days of the week for the timeliness rule

deploymentId.description: The ID of the deployment for the alert rule

taskDurationSeconds.description: The duration of the Task in seconds (minimum 60)

deploymentId.description: The Deployment ID. Required for DAG and TAG entity types.

entityId.description: The ID of the entity to assign the role to. For DAG entity type, this is the dag_id. For TAG entity type, this is the tag value.

entityType.description: The type of entity to assign the role to

role.description: The role to assign to the entity

cloudProvider.description: Node pool cloud provider. Allowed values: `AWS`, `GCP`, `AZURE`.

isDefault.description: Whether the node pool is the default node pool of the cluster

create.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

delete.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.

update.description: A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

isSecret.description: Whether Environment variable is a secret

allowedIpAddressRanges.description: The allowed IP address ranges for remote execution

remoteApiUrl.description: The URL for the remote API

taskLogUrlPattern.description: The URL pattern for task logs

hibernationSpec.description: Hibernation configuration for the deployment. The deployment will hibernate according to the schedules defined in this configuration. To remove the hibernation configuration, set scaling*spec to null.

override.description: Hibernation override configuration. Set to null to remove the override.

schedules.description: List of hibernation schedules. Set to null to remove all schedules.

astroMachine.description: Worker queue Astro machine value - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments. Allowed values: `A5`, `A10`, `A20`, `A40`, `A60`, `A120`, `A160`.

nodePoolId.description: Worker queue Node pool identifier - required for 'HYBRID' deployments

provider.description:
The provider type for the astro package. By default, resources use package-wide configuration
settings, however an explicit `Provider` instance may be created and passed during resource
construction to achieve fine-grained programmatic control over provider settings. See the
[documentation](https://www.pulumi.com/docs/reference/programming-model/#providers) for more information.

token.description: Astro API Token. Can be set with an `ASTRO_API_TOKEN` env var.

astronomer:index/cluster:Cluster.description: Cluster resource. If creating multiple clusters, add a delay between each cluster creation to avoid cluster creation limiting errors.

cloudProvider.description: Cluster cloud provider - if changed, the cluster will be recreated. Allowed values: `AWS`, `GCP`, `AZURE`.

drRegion.description: The secondary region for Disaster Recovery. Required when `isDrEnabled` is true. Cannot be changed once set.

drSecondaryVpcCidr.description: Secondary CIDR for pod networking in the DR region (AWS only). Cannot be changed once set.

drVpcSubnetRange.description: The VPC subnet range for the Disaster Recovery region. Only valid when `isDrEnabled` is true. Cannot be changed once set.

enableReplicationTimeControl.description: Whether to enable S3 Replication Time Control for Disaster Recovery. Only valid when `isDrEnabled` is true (AWS only).

isDrEnabled.description: Whether Disaster Recovery is enabled on the cluster. Only supported for AWS clusters. Can only be enabled at cluster creation time. Can be set to `false` to disable DR on an existing cluster.

isFailedOver.description: Whether the cluster is currently failed over to the DR region. Set to `true` to trigger failover; set to `false` to fail back.

podSubnetRange.description: Cluster pod subnet range - required for 'GCP' clusters. If changed, the cluster will be recreated.

region.description: Cluster region - if changed, the cluster will be recreated.

servicePeeringRange.description: Cluster service peering range - required for 'GCP' clusters. If changed, the cluster will be recreated.

serviceSubnetRange.description: Cluster service subnet range - required for 'GCP' clusters. If changed, the cluster will be recreated.

vpcSubnetRange.description: Cluster VPC subnet range. If changed, the cluster will be recreated.

cloudProvider.description: Cluster cloud provider - if changed, the cluster will be recreated. Allowed values: `AWS`, `GCP`, `AZURE`.

drSecondaryVpcCidr.description: Secondary CIDR for pod networking in the DR region (AWS only). Cannot be changed once set.

drVpcSubnetRange.description: The VPC subnet range for the Disaster Recovery region. Only valid when `isDrEnabled` is true. Cannot be changed once set.

enableReplicationTimeControl.description: Whether to enable S3 Replication Time Control for Disaster Recovery. Only valid when `isDrEnabled` is true (AWS only).

isFailedOver.description: Whether the cluster is currently failed over to the DR region. Set to `true` to trigger failover; set to `false` to fail back.

podSubnetRange.description: Cluster pod subnet range - required for 'GCP' clusters. If changed, the cluster will be recreated.

region.description: Cluster region - if changed, the cluster will be recreated.

servicePeeringRange.description: Cluster service peering range - required for 'GCP' clusters. If changed, the cluster will be recreated.

serviceSubnetRange.description: Cluster service subnet range - required for 'GCP' clusters. If changed, the cluster will be recreated.

vpcSubnetRange.description: Cluster VPC subnet range. If changed, the cluster will be recreated.

createdAt.description: The time the custom role was created

createdBy.description: The subject who created the custom role

restrictedWorkspaceIds.description: The IDs of Workspaces that the custom role is restricted to

scopeType.description: The custom role's scope (DEPLOYMENT or DAG)

updatedAt.description: The time the custom role was last updated

updatedBy.description: The subject who last updated the custom role

cloudProvider.description: Deployment cloud provider - required for 'STANDARD' deployments. If changing this value, the deployment will be recreated in the new cloud provider. Allowed values: `AWS`, `GCP`, `AZURE`.

clusterId.description: Deployment cluster identifier - required for 'HYBRID' and 'DEDICATED' deployments. If changing this value, the deployment will be recreated in the new cluster

defaultTaskPodCpu.description: Deployment default task pod CPU - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

defaultTaskPodMemory.description: Deployment default task pod memory - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

desiredWorkloadIdentity.description: Deployment's desired workload identity. The Terraform provider will use this provided workload identity to create the Deployment. If it is not provided the workload identity will be assigned automatically.

environmentVariables.description: Deployment environment variables. When importing a deployment, you must include all environment variables in your configuration. Any variables not specified will be deleted on the next apply. Secret values must be re-entered as the API does not return them.

isDagDeployEnabled.description: Whether DAG deploy is enabled - Changing this value may disrupt your deployment. Read more at https://docs.astronomer.io/astro/deploy-dags#enable-or-disable-dag-only-deploys-on-a-deployment

isDevelopmentMode.description: Deployment development mode - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments. If changing from 'False' to 'True', the deployment will be recreated

isHighAvailability.description: Deployment high availability - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

originalAstroRuntimeVersion.description: Deployment's original Astro Runtime version. The Terraform provider will use this provided Astro runtime version to create the Deployment. If not provided, defaults to the current Astro runtime version. The Astro runtime version can be updated with your Astro project Dockerfile, but if this value is changed, the Deployment will be recreated with this new Astro runtime version.

region.description: Deployment region - required for 'STANDARD' deployments. If changing this value, the deployment will be recreated in the new region

remoteExecution.description: Deployment remote execution configuration - only for 'DEDICATED' deployments

resourceQuotaCpu.description: Deployment resource quota CPU - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

resourceQuotaMemory.description: Deployment resource quota memory - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

scalingSpec.description: Deployment scaling spec - only for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

schedulerAu.description: Deployment scheduler AU - required for 'HYBRID' deployments

schedulerReplicas.description: Deployment scheduler replicas - required for 'HYBRID' deployments

schedulerSize.description: Deployment scheduler size - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments. Allowed values: `SMALL`, `MEDIUM`, `LARGE`, `EXTRALARGE`.

taskPodNodePoolId.description: Deployment task pod node pool identifier - required if executor is 'KUBERNETES' and type is 'HYBRID'

type.description: Deployment type - if changing this value, the deployment will be recreated with the new type

workerQueues.description: Deployment worker queues - required for deployments with 'CELERY' executor. For 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments, use astro*machine. For 'HYBRID' deployments, use node*pool*id.

workloadIdentity.description: Deployment workload identity. This value can be changed via the Astro API if applicable.

workspaceId.description: Deployment workspace identifier - if changing this value, the deployment will be recreated in the new workspace

cloudProvider.description: Deployment cloud provider - required for 'STANDARD' deployments. If changing this value, the deployment will be recreated in the new cloud provider. Allowed values: `AWS`, `GCP`, `AZURE`.

clusterId.description: Deployment cluster identifier - required for 'HYBRID' and 'DEDICATED' deployments. If changing this value, the deployment will be recreated in the new cluster

defaultTaskPodCpu.description: Deployment default task pod CPU - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

defaultTaskPodMemory.description: Deployment default task pod memory - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

desiredWorkloadIdentity.description: Deployment's desired workload identity. The Terraform provider will use this provided workload identity to create the Deployment. If it is not provided the workload identity will be assigned automatically.

environmentVariables.description: Deployment environment variables. When importing a deployment, you must include all environment variables in your configuration. Any variables not specified will be deleted on the next apply. Secret values must be re-entered as the API does not return them.

isDagDeployEnabled.description: Whether DAG deploy is enabled - Changing this value may disrupt your deployment. Read more at https://docs.astronomer.io/astro/deploy-dags#enable-or-disable-dag-only-deploys-on-a-deployment

isDevelopmentMode.description: Deployment development mode - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments. If changing from 'False' to 'True', the deployment will be recreated

isHighAvailability.description: Deployment high availability - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

originalAstroRuntimeVersion.description: Deployment's original Astro Runtime version. The Terraform provider will use this provided Astro runtime version to create the Deployment. If not provided, defaults to the current Astro runtime version. The Astro runtime version can be updated with your Astro project Dockerfile, but if this value is changed, the Deployment will be recreated with this new Astro runtime version.

region.description: Deployment region - required for 'STANDARD' deployments. If changing this value, the deployment will be recreated in the new region

remoteExecution.description: Deployment remote execution configuration - only for 'DEDICATED' deployments

resourceQuotaCpu.description: Deployment resource quota CPU - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

resourceQuotaMemory.description: Deployment resource quota memory - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

scalingSpec.description: Deployment scaling spec - only for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

schedulerAu.description: Deployment scheduler AU - required for 'HYBRID' deployments

schedulerReplicas.description: Deployment scheduler replicas - required for 'HYBRID' deployments

schedulerSize.description: Deployment scheduler size - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments. Allowed values: `SMALL`, `MEDIUM`, `LARGE`, `EXTRALARGE`.

taskPodNodePoolId.description: Deployment task pod node pool identifier - required if executor is 'KUBERNETES' and type is 'HYBRID'

type.description: Deployment type - if changing this value, the deployment will be recreated with the new type

workerQueues.description: Deployment worker queues - required for deployments with 'CELERY' executor. For 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments, use astro*machine. For 'HYBRID' deployments, use node*pool*id.

workspaceId.description: Deployment workspace identifier - if changing this value, the deployment will be recreated in the new workspace

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering Deployment resources.

cloudProvider.description: Deployment cloud provider - required for 'STANDARD' deployments. If changing this value, the deployment will be recreated in the new cloud provider. Allowed values: `AWS`, `GCP`, `AZURE`.

clusterId.description: Deployment cluster identifier - required for 'HYBRID' and 'DEDICATED' deployments. If changing this value, the deployment will be recreated in the new cluster

defaultTaskPodCpu.description: Deployment default task pod CPU - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

defaultTaskPodMemory.description: Deployment default task pod memory - required for 'STANDARD' and 'DEDICATED' deployments

desiredWorkloadIdentity.description: Deployment's desired workload identity. The Terraform provider will use this provided workload identity to create the Deployment. If it is not provided the workload identity will be assigned automatically.

environmentVariables.description: Deployment environment variables. When importing a deployment, you must include all environment variables in your configuration. Any variables not specified will be deleted on the next apply. Secret values must be re-entered as the API does not return them.

isDagDeployEnabled.description: Whether DAG deploy is enabled - Changing this value may disrupt your deployment. Read more at https://docs.astronomer.io/astro/deploy-dags#enable-or-disable-dag-only-deploys-on-a-deployment

astronomer:index/team:Team.description: Creates and manages a team and its members. Astro permissions are hierarchical (organization, workspace, deployment, then DAG). Declare roles at each applicable parent scope as well as nested scopes, not only at the leaf, so Terraform state matches the API.

dagRoles.description: The DAG roles to assign to the team. Each role grants permissions to a specific DAG or DAGs with a specific tag within a deployment. Each deployment referenced in `dagRoles` must also have a corresponding entry in `deploymentRoles` (for example `DEPLOYMENT_ACCESSOR`).

deploymentRoles.description: The roles to assign to the Deployments. Each `deploymentId` must belong to a workspace that also appears in `workspaceRoles`. Required for any deployment referenced in `dagRoles`.

isIdpManaged.description: Whether the Team is managed by an identity provider

memberIds.description: The IDs of the users to add to the Team

organizationRole.description: The role to assign to the Organization

rolesCount.description: Number of roles assigned to the Team

workspaceRoles.description: The roles to assign to the Workspaces. When you set `deploymentRoles` or `dagRoles`, include each deployment's parent workspace here (any workspace role), so Terraform state matches the API.

dagRoles.description: The DAG roles to assign to the team. Each role grants permissions to a specific DAG or DAGs with a specific tag within a deployment. Each deployment referenced in `dagRoles` must also have a corresponding entry in `deploymentRoles` (for example `DEPLOYMENT_ACCESSOR`).

deploymentRoles.description: The roles to assign to the Deployments. Each `deploymentId` must belong to a workspace that also appears in `workspaceRoles`. Required for any deployment referenced in `dagRoles`.

astronomer:index/teamMembership:TeamMemb.description: Manages a single user's membership in an Astro team. Use this resource instead of the `memberIds` attribute on `astronomer.Team` when you need to manage memberships independently or across multiple state files. **Conflict note:** Do not use both `astro_team.member_ids` and `astronomer.TeamMembership` for the same team. Both write to the same API state and will conflict on apply.

astronomer:index/getNotificationChannels.description:
Notification Channels data source

## Example Usage

<!--Start PulumiCodeChooser -->
```yaml
variables:
  exampleNotificationChannels:
    fn::invoke:
      function: astronomer:getNotificationChannels
      arguments: {}
  notificationChannelIdsExample:
    fn::invoke:
      function: astronomer:getNotificationChannels
      arguments:
        notificationChannelIds:
          - cm7dz31ye00cz01n1en2xilut
  workspaceIdsExample:
    fn::invoke:
      function: astronomer:getNotificationChannels
      arguments:
        workspaceIds:
          - clx42sxw501gl01o0gjenthnh
  deploymentIdsExample:
    fn::invoke:
      function: astronomer:getNotificationChannels
      arguments:
        deploymentIds:
          - clx44jyu001m201m5dzsbexqr
  notificationChannelTypesExample:
    fn::invoke:
      function: astronomer:getNotificationChannels
      arguments:
        notificationChannelTypes:
          - SLACK
          - EMAIL
  entityTypeExample:
    fn::invoke:
      function: astronomer:getNotificationChannels
      arguments:
        entityType: DEPLOYMENT
outputs:
  # Output the API tokens using pulumi up
  notificationChannels: ${exampleNotificationChannels}
```
<!--End PulumiCodeChooser -->
